historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.

historicEvents.title: The Black Hawk War
historicEvents.description: Convinced that a group of Native American tribes were hostile, The United States formed a frontier militia to stop them in their tracks. Even though Black Hawk was hoping to avoid bloodshed while trying to resettle on tribal land, U.S. officials opened fire on the Native Americans. Black Hawk then responded to this confrontation by successfully attacking the militia at the Battle of Stillman's Run and then left northward. After a few months the militia caught up with Black Hawk and his men and defeated them at the Battle of Wisconsin Heights. While being weakened by hunger, injuries and desertion, Black Hawk and the rest of the many native survivors retreated towards the Mississippi. Unfortunately, Black Hawk and other leaders were later captured when they surrendered to the US forces and were then imprisoned for a year.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Battle at Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The Battle of Gettysburg involved the largest number of casualties of the entire Civil war and is often described as the war's turning point. Between 46,000 and 51,000 soldiers lost their lives during the three-day Battle. To honor the fallen soldiers, President Abraham Lincoln read his historic Gettysburg Address and helped those listening by redefining the purpose of the war.

en.lifeSummary: Lewis Utter was born on 2 February 1815, in New York, United States. He had at least 2 sons and 5 daughters with Delilah Day. He died on 5 August 1874, in Worcester, Otsego, New York, United States, at the age of 59, and was buried in Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, New York, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Lewis Utterは1815年2月2日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州で生まれた。彼はDelilah Dayとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼は1874年8月5日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴウオルセスターで59歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴウオルセスターマプル グローブ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Maple Grove Cemetery Worcester Otsego Co: 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 워스스터 마플 그로브 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: Lewis Utter(은)는 1815년 2월 2일 미국 뉴욕 주에서 태어났다. 그는 Delilah Day(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1874년 8월 5일 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 워스스터에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 워스스터 마플 그로브 세멧리에 묻혔다.

ru.Maple Grove Cemetery Worcester Otsego Co: мапл гров кеметири, ворсестир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Lewis Utter родился 2 февраля 1815 г. в местности Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей от Delilah Day. Он умер 5 августа 1874 г. в местности ворсестир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности мапл гров кеметири, ворсестир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.
